summary: End-to-end testing of React → Oracle APEX Generator. Backend: 5/5 pytest tests passed (versions endpoint, generate with valid zip, version 22.2, non-zip rejection, runs listing). SQL output validated to contain all required APEX PL/SQL calls (set_security_group_id, g_flow_id, create_page, create_page_plug, create_page_item, create_page_button, create_page_process, wwv_flow_imp_shared.create_app_static_file), NATIVE_IR for reports, NATIVE_JET_CHART for dashboards, floatingLabel template option for forms (verified on both 24.2 and 22.2). Frontend: all UI flows pass — page loads, sidebar tabs visible, upload zone, config inputs (workspace/app-id/version/build toggle), generate disabled→enabled toggle, file upload shows filename + Ready badge, generate triggers API and renders syntax-highlighted SQL in output panel, Download triggers .sql file (apex_205247_24_2.sql), APEX Pages tab shows 3 cards (SalesDashboard/Dashboard, UsersReport/Interactive Report, UserForm/Form, 4 items), Export panel displays download button. No console errors.

integration_issues.issue: 'Copied!' text state was not observed after click in headless browser; navigator.clipboard.writeText likely rejected without user gesture/permission. Code path is correct — falls back to toast.error. Verify manually in a real browser.

critical_code_review_comments: apex_generator.py: q-quoted CLOB delimiter uses '[' and ']' — if generated CSS contains the literal sequence ']'' the escape `.replace("]'", "] '")` only handles the closing pattern '][quote]; safer to use a randomized delimiter like q'~...~' or check for ']'' specifically.
Generator.jsx line 79/126: result.apex_version.replace('.', '_') only replaces first dot — acceptable for X.Y versions, but fragile if format changes.
server.py _run_build uses synchronous subprocess inside async endpoint — will block the event loop for up to 8 minutes when run_build=true. Consider asyncio.create_subprocess_exec or run_in_executor.
apex_parser EMPTY_FORM_RE uses non-greedy [^}]+ — nested braces in default values will truncate field detection (low impact for typical React state objects).
seed_data_creation: In-memory ZIP fixture built inside test_apex_generator.py with UserForm.jsx (form), UsersReport.tsx (report), SalesDashboard.jsx (recharts dashboard). No DB seed required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_apex_generator.py — run via `pytest backend/tests/test_apex_generator.py -v`. Fixture builds the React zip in-memory so /tmp/sample.zip is not required. Clipboard copy 'Copied!' badge needs real-browser verification, not headless.
